reddit_title: Got hit by a semi-truck in Lubbock, TX — do I actually need a lawyer or can I handle the insurance claim myself?
reddit_body:
Truck accident cases in Texas are a different animal compared to regular car accident claims, and it's worth understanding why before deciding to go it alone. Commercial trucking companies are regulated by the FMCSA, which means there are detailed logs, inspection records, and electronic black box data that could be critical to your case — but trucking companies are only required to preserve that data for a limited time, and some may not go out of their way to keep it once they know litigation isn't imminent.

Liability in truck crashes is also more layered. Depending on the situation, you could have claims against the truck driver, the trucking company, a freight broker, a cargo loader, or even a maintenance contractor. Insurance policies on commercial trucks are also much larger than personal auto policies, which means insurers fight harder to minimize payouts. Having someone who knows how to navigate FMCSA regulations and multi-party liability makes a real difference.

If you're in the Lubbock area and trying to figure out your next steps, it's worth at least talking to a truck accident attorney before making any decisions — most offer free consultations. A service like wreckmatch.com can match you with experienced truck accident lawyers in Texas without any obligation, so you can get real answers about your specific situation before committing to anything.
